name: CircuitNet
description: Large-scale open dataset with 20,000+ chip designs from commercial EDA tools. Provides routing congestion maps, IR drop heatmaps, DRC hotspots, and timing slack data. The canonical benchmark for ML-based physical design prediction.

name: ISPD 2018 Initial Detailed Routing Contest
description: Official benchmark suite from the ISPD 2018 detailed routing contest. Contains industrial-strength designs with complex multi-cut via rules, preferred routing directions, and spacing constraints representative of 7nm-class processes.

name: OpenROAD Flow Benchmarks
description: A curated set of open-source RTL designs (RISC-V cores, memory controllers, DSP blocks) used to benchmark the OpenROAD flow across SKY130, GF180MCU, and ASAP7 PDKs. Covers a wide range of design sizes and complexities.

name: SKY130 Open Analog Design Examples
description: Collection of open-source analog circuit designs (op-amps, LDOs, PLLs, ring oscillators, bandgaps) taped out or validated on the SkyWater 130nm process. Includes schematics, layout, simulations, and post-layout extracted netlists.

name: ALIGN Analog Layout Benchmarks
description: Benchmark netlists for analog layout automation research, including folded-cascode op-amps, telescopic op-amps, current mirrors, and comparators. Each circuit includes a reference manual layout and performance specs for evaluating automated layout quality.

name: MAGICAL Layout Benchmarks
description: Layout benchmark suite from the MAGICAL project covering operational amplifiers, bandgap references, and switched-capacitor circuits. Includes both TSMC 65nm and TSMC 16nm FinFET target technologies.

name: AutoCkt Analog Sizing Dataset
description: Simulation dataset of op-amp and two-stage amplifier SPICE simulations generated for training and evaluating RL-based circuit sizing agents. Contains device sizes, bias conditions, and corresponding performance metrics (gain, bandwidth, phase margin, power).

name: PROBE3.0 Design-Technology Pathfinding Benchmarks
description: Benchmark suite for evaluating standard cell library design, routing resource estimation, and PPA tradeoffs across technology nodes. Used for design-technology co-optimization research and reproducible pathfinding studies.

name: CircuitFormer
description: 130,409 ngspice-simulated SPICE netlists on the open-source SkyWater SKY130 PDK, unified from three upstream sources into a single HuggingFace-loadable schema with deterministic topology-aware train/validation/test splits. Designed as a supervised (SPICE netlist → performance metrics) corpus for training of models for analog circuit design.

name: AnalogGym: An Open and Practical Testing Suite for Analog Circuit Synthesis
description: AnalogGym encompasses 30 circuit topologies in five key categories: sensing front ends, voltage references, AMPs, low dropout regulators (LDOs), and phase-locked loops (PLLs). Among these, the LDOs and AMPs support the open-source [Ngspice](https://ngspice.sourceforge.io/) simulator and the [SkyWater](https://github.com/google/skywater-pdk)  process design kit (PDK), allowing for greater accessibility and reproducibility.
